response.justification: Szabo is a strong senior engineer with solid backend systems expertise and recent AI-agent infrastructure experience, but fundamentally lacks the executive-leadership credentials and fintech domain knowledge this CTO role demands. The role explicitly requires 5+ years in senior technical leadership managing organizations at scale; his resume shows no CTO, VP Engineering, or leadership positions—only individual-contributor staff roles and a side-project founder role. Additionally, there is zero fintech or regulated-systems experience, which is critical for architecting working-capital management, credit-risk systems, and compliance infrastructure.
strengths: Recent agentic-AI infrastructure experience at re:cinq with MCP code generators and hands-on familiarity with AI-agent workflows
Proven real-time, high-scale backend systems expertise including millions-of-requests-per-second processing and event-driven architecture design at Dunnhumby
Deep full-stack technical breadth across multiple languages and domains, demonstrating ability to navigate complex architectural challenges
concerns: No demonstrated senior technical leadership experience; all roles are individual-contributor or solo-founder positions with no evidence of scaling teams or mentoring a senior technical layer
Complete absence of fintech domain expertise—no experience with credit systems, payment flows, working-capital algorithms, regulatory compliance, or financial infrastructure

AI/agent production experience is recent (1 year) and unvalidated; no evidence of operating multi-agent systems at scale, managing production failure modes, or orchestrating stateful agent feedback loops

key_factors.factor: No executive technical leadership track record
Missing fintech and regulated-systems domain knowledge
Recent but unproven agentic-AI infrastructure exposure
